search.dialog.title: Search

@search.dialog.title.description: Title shown at the top of the whole-drive file Search dialog. Single word naming the feature.

@search.runHint.description: Subtle hint in the right side of the search input, shown when auto-apply is off and the typed query hasn't run yet. Tells the user the search runs on Enter.

search.action.showAll.label: Show all in main window

@search.action.showAll.label.description: Primary footer button in the Search dialog. Promotes the current search results into the active file pane so the user can browse them like a folder. Activated by the keyboard shortcut shown next to it.

search.action.showAll.tooltip: Open the search results in the active pane

@search.action.showAll.tooltip.description: Hover tooltip for the 'Show all in main window' button in the Search dialog.

search.action.goToFile.label: Go to file

@search.action.goToFile.label.description: Secondary footer button in the Search dialog. Opens the file under the cursor by navigating the active pane to it. Activated by the Enter key shown next to it.

search.action.goToFile.tooltip: Open the file in the active pane

@search.action.goToFile.tooltip.description: Hover tooltip for the 'Go to file' button in the Search dialog.

@search.recent.runAria.description: Accessibility label for a recent-search chip in the Search dialog. Read aloud by screen readers; activating the chip re-runs that past search.

placeholders.mode: the search mode of the past search, already translated (for example AI, Regex, or Filename)
placeholders.query: the text the user searched for, shown verbatim and not translated

search.systemDirExclude.default: Excludes common system and build folders

@search.systemDirExclude.default.description: Placeholder tooltip on the 'Hide boring folders' option in the Search dialog's scope popover, shown briefly before the full folder list loads. 'build folders' are folders like node_modules that hold generated or downloaded code.

search.systemDirExclude.heading: These folders are hidden:

@search.systemDirExclude.heading.description: Heading inside the 'Hide boring folders' tooltip in the Search dialog's scope popover. Introduces the list of folder names that the search skips. The folder names themselves are not translated.

search.notAFolderToast: Search results aren''t a folder. Paste into a real folder instead.

@search.notAFolderToast.description: Toast shown when the user tries a paste, new-folder, or rename action while a pane is showing search results. Search results are a read-only view, not a real folder, so those actions can't apply there.

search.searchableFolder.disabledTooltip: Current folder is search results, which isn''t searchable. Open a real folder first.

@search.searchableFolder.disabledTooltip.description: Tooltip on the disabled 'Use current folder' button in the Search dialog's scope popover. Explains that the active pane is showing search results (a read-only view), so there's no real folder to search inside.

search.imageResults.title: Text in images

@search.imageResults.title.description: Heading for the section of the Search dialog that shows images whose OCR-read text matches the query, rendered as a thumbnail grid below the filename results.

@search.imageResults.count.description: Count shown next to the "Text in images" heading when every match is displayed.

placeholders.totalText: the number of matching images, already formatted for display (for example 1,240)

@search.imageResults.countCapped.description: Count shown next to the "Text in images" heading when there are more matches than the grid displays at once.

placeholders.shownText: how many images the grid shows, already formatted (for example 48)
placeholders.totalText: the total number of matching images, already formatted (for example 1,240)

search.imageResults.off: Image indexing is off. Turn on "Index image contents" in Settings to search the text inside your images.

@search.imageResults.off.description: Shown in the "Text in images" section when the image-content indexing feature is turned off. "Index image contents" is the exact label of the Settings toggle (Settings > Behavior > File system watching); keep it matching.

search.imageResults.indexing: Still indexing images, so results may be incomplete.

@search.imageResults.indexing.description: Shown while a background pass is still reading text from this drive''s images, warning the user the image results aren''t complete yet.

search.imageResults.notIndexed: This drive''s images aren''t indexed yet. They''ll become searchable once indexing runs.

@search.imageResults.notIndexed.description: Shown when image indexing is on but no images on this drive have been read yet, so there''s nothing to search. Distinct from a genuine no-match.

@search.imageResults.empty.description: Shown when this drive''s images have been indexed but none of them contain the searched text (a genuine no-match, not "not indexed yet").

search.imageResults.networkOff: Turn on image indexing for this network drive in Settings to search the text inside its photos.

@search.imageResults.networkOff.description: Shown in the "Text in images" section when searching a network (SMB) drive that hasn''t been opted into image indexing. Points the user to the per-drive opt-in in Settings > Behavior > File system watching > Image search.

search.imageResults.paused: This drive is disconnected, so these are the photos indexed so far.

@search.imageResults.paused.description: Shown when a network drive disconnected mid-indexing: what was already indexed stays searchable, and indexing resumes when the drive reconnects.

@search.snapshot.defaultLabel.description: Fallback breadcrumb and tab title for a saved search-results view when there's no query text to name it. Same word as the dialog title.

@search.snapshot.cappedLabel.description: Breadcrumb and tab title for a saved search-results view when the result set was too large to keep entirely, so only the first N of the total M matches are shown. The base label is the original search name.

placeholders.label: the base search label (the query or AI summary), shown verbatim
placeholders.capText: how many matches are kept, already formatted for display (for example 1,000)
placeholders.totalText: the total number of matches found, already formatted for display (for example 5,432)
